description_long: This bollard features a tall, cylindrical stone shaft supporting an ornate, shield-shaped upper section with intricate relief carvings on its faces. The top is capped with a dark, steeply pitched, triangular roof that gives it a distinctive, almost architectural appearance. The carvings on the upper section appear to depict heraldic or decorative motifs, adding a historical or ceremonial character. The stone surface is weathered, with visible discoloration and patina, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The base is simple and cylindrical, contrasting with the elaborate upper portion. This object would likely serve as a decorative or symbolic barrier in a historic urban setting, such as a plaza, park, or near a significant building.
description: A tall, cylindrical stone bollard with an ornate, shield-shaped upper section featuring relief carvings and a dark, pitched triangular cap. The weathered surface and decorative details suggest a historic or ceremonial function.
description_view_0: Front view shows the shield-shaped upper section with detailed relief carvings and the dark, pitched cap clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the triangular profile of the cap and the cylindrical shaft, with less detail visible on the carvings.
description_view_2: Opposite front view displays another face of the ornate upper section, with different relief carvings and the same cap design.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again highlights the triangular cap profile and the cylindrical base, with carvings partially visible.
